title: The TRUTH Behind George W. Bush's Success
summary: A brief discussion about George W. Bush's membership in Skull and Bones at Yale, highlighting his exceptional social memory skills and his father George H.W. Bush's involvement in the same secret society.

key_claims.claim: George W. Bush could remember the names and birthplaces of all recruits during a Skull and Bones initiation exercise, while most people only remembered four or five
George W. Bush made C's in physics and chemistry but was considered a social genius
George H.W. Bush was also a member of Skull and Bones with the nickname 'Magog', reserved for the member who had slept with the most women
